title: Dubrueilia lanceolata
taxonomy: Dubrueilia lanceolata is classified as a species within the genus Dubrueilia. This genus is further classified within the family Gentianaceae, commonly known as the gentian family. The family Gentianaceae is part of the order Gentianales, which is a group of flowering plants. The order Gentianales is part of the clade Asterids, a major division of flowering plants. The Asterids clade is part of the larger clade Angiosperm, which encompasses all flowering plants.

parts: I don't have information about Dubrueilia lanceolata.
preparations: Dubrueilia lanceolata, also known as Devil's Claw, is a plant with a long history of traditional use in herbal medicine. In herbal preparations, the roots of the plant are typically used, which contain a variety of bioactive compounds, including iridoids and glycosides. These compounds are believed to have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making the plant a popular ingredient in remedies for arthritis, fibromyalgia, and other musculoskeletal conditions. Dubrueilia lanceolata herbal preparations are often available in capsule, tablet, or liquid form, and are also used in traditional African medicine. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Dubrueilia lanceolata for medicinal purposes.
